input.background: Two studies report results with P-values of <0.001 and =0.02. The question explores why it is not necessarily true that the former study has found a stronger effect than the latter, as discussed in section 16.3.6.
input.question: What other factors can influence the P value for continuous variables?

output.answer: For continuous variables, the P value also depends upon the variability (see exercise 9.6).
